title: CSS for Web Performance Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating fast and responsive web experiences using CSS.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a high-performance web application in CSS?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using simple and efficient class selectors (like .nav-link) to avoid complex CSS rules.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using CSS minification compared to writing raw CSS?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It helps browsers load the site significantly faster by removing extra spaces and comments.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'Critical CSS' inside a professional web application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
To extract the essential CSS for 'Above the Fold' content and place it directly in the HTML <head> for the initial view.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid animating 'layout' properties like 'margin' or 'top' in your professional CSS?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They trigger the browser's expensive layout re-calculation, which can be much slower than using GPU-accelerated ones like 'transform'.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for performance?
options: Commenting every single line of code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'content-visibility: auto;' to allow the browser to skip rendering elements not yet visible on the screen.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
